extra.reason: Superseded by openspec change gate-signal-persistence-foundation (created same session). The work is now tracked via openspec design.md + future /work-breakdown beads, not as a single bead under the audit epic. The signal-persistence foundation enables both the staleness feature's learning loop AND the other 9 audit-revisions; promoting it from one of 10 children to its own change reflects that scope and surfaces it for earlier execution.

Signal-capture portion implemented and shipped via the gate-signal-persistence-foundation. The original audit finding for both beads centered on missing signal persistence (operating rule 2 of gate-design.md), and that piece is now resolved. Secondary suggestions from the audit (rule-file pointer for context_burn_detector, --proceed waiver-reason capture for outcome_assertion_gate) are smaller follow-on items that can be picked up under the standard waiver convention work — not blocking.

Signal-capture portion implemented and shipped via the gate-signal-persistence-foundation. The original audit finding for both beads centered on missing signal persistence (operating rule 2 of gate-design.md), and that piece is now resolved. Secondary suggestions from the audit (rule-file pointer for context_burn_detector, --proceed waiver-reason capture for outcome_assertion_gate) are smaller follow-on items that can be picked up under the standard waiver convention work — not blocking.

Closed by review_gate revise. The _WARN_NO_REVIEW message now includes the rationale (what review catches) and the two satisfaction signals (subagent_type or review-word). Signal capture added at both decision branches via the gate-signal-persistence-foundation. Friction-analyst's specific recommendation implemented in full.

discovery-close-gate revised: 90-day mtime cutoff removed (petrification), within-session dedup added (anti-fatigue), global-transparency header added to prompts, signal capture at all three branches. All three audit findings resolved in one commit.

enforce_named_agents revised: 'NEVER' absolutism softened to 'almost never, and here's the escape' with two specific legitimate cases named (one-off, user-requested probe), the throwaway-name pattern (name='oneoff' or 'probe') documented. Signal capture added at all four decision branches. The dynamic countdown follow-on (replace static '30 seconds' with live timer) is noted in commit but deferred — the main UX concern is addressed by the softened text.

test_oracle_brief_gate revised: hard deny converted to 'ask' with documented 'say proceed to skip' escape. Standard convention now matches tdd-gate, outcome_assertion_gate, discovery-gate. Signal capture at both decision branches. Friction-analyst's recommendation implemented.

oracle_downgrade_warning_gate revised: unverifiable honor-system text replaced with mechanically-verifiable check (.agent/runtime/test-oracle-brief.md modified in last 24h). Two explicit paths forward in the denial: update the brief OR say 'proceed' (captured). Signal capture at both decision branches. Integrator's mock-bureaucracy-risk finding addressed.

implementation_echo_test_gate revised: per-file # oracle: <reason> override implemented (Python and JS/TS comment forms). Hard deny converted to 'ask' with both rewrite-the-test and override-via-comment paths documented in the message. Override reasons captured as labeled training data. Signal capture at all three decision branches. Friction-analyst's recommendation implemented in full.

validate_no_shirking revised: 33 regex patterns categorized into 8 named anti-pattern classes (pre-existing, attribution-deflection, unrelated, infrastructure-blame, deferral, scope-limitation, acceptance-evasion, dismissal); denial message names the matched category with a one-line rationale and links to outcome-ownership.md; signal capture at all 3 decision branches including waiver-accepted entries that capture user-overrides as labeled training data. The half-life mechanism (gate-design.md Operating Rule 1) is now mechanically queryable via gate_signal_query.py --gate validate_no_shirking. Adversary's full recommendation implemented. This is the highest-priority gate revise from the audit and the last open bead in epic claude-workflow-setup-3ky.

All 10 audit-revision children closed. The gate audit identified 11 issues (10 specific gate revises + 1 system-wide signal persistence which was promoted to its own openspec change gate-signal-persistence-foundation). All resolved in this session:

- bfb2555 — bureaucracy principle file (the diagnostic vocabulary)
- b4d2732 — gate-design rule (the operational complement)
- f85164c — spec_id_enforcement value validation (mock bureaucracy fix)
- 5b6d934 — gate-signal-persistence foundation (Rule 2 infrastructure)
- 69f340e, 32d0f6f, a848579, fac86e3 — signal migration across 6 gates
- 1a929b2, 4bbb07f, c9f169b, 2dab359, 8a0f8bd, 853b4ce, dcbd932 — per-gate revises

14 of 14 gates now signal-capturing. Half-life mechanism queryable via gate_signal_query.py. The principle file and gate-design rule both eat their own dogfood — applied to themselves and to every gate in the repo.

Test bead from gate_signal_monitor --file-beads end-to-end verification. Synthetic _test_fake_gate signal entries injected to trigger the bead-filing path. Both filing and dedup verified working. Closing because the test pattern is gone.
